metadata.title: EU GMP & EMA Manufacturing and Quality Requirements
eu_gmp_chapters: Chapter 8: Complaints, Quality Defects and Product Recalls

rules.requirement: Manufacturing Authorisation holder must manufacture medicinal products fit for intended use, complying with Marketing Authorisation requirements
Pharmaceutical Quality System must incorporate GMP and Quality Risk Management, be fully documented, and its effectiveness monitored
Product and process knowledge must be managed throughout all lifecycle stages
No batch sold or supplied before Qualified Person certification that each batch produced and controlled per Marketing Authorisation
rules.fda_equivalent: 21 CFR 211.22 - Quality Control Unit responsibilities, but no QP role

rules.requirement: Annual Product Quality Review (PQR) required for all authorised products including export-only, covering 12 specified elements
Quality Risk Management must be systematic, based on scientific knowledge, and commensurate with level of risk per ICH Q9
Senior management periodic review of PQS required
Quality Manual or equivalent documentation must describe the quality management system including management responsibilities
Qualified Person(s) must be designated per Article 51 of Directive 2001/83/EC with specific education qualifications per Article 49
rules.fda_equivalent: No equivalent role in FDA system

rules.requirement: Heads of Production and Quality Control must be independent from each other
Organisation chart required showing relationships between Production, QC, QA heads and QP positions
Medical examination required upon recruitment for all personnel
rules.fda_equivalent: 21 CFR 211.28 only requires health reporting, not mandatory medical exam

rules.requirement: Consultant records must include name, address, qualifications, and type of service
QP must certify each batch in a register before any release, covering full qualitative analysis and quantitative analysis of all active substances for imported products
Dedicated facilities required when toxicological evaluation does not support controllable risk (e.g., beta-lactams) or residue limits cannot be determined by validated method
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA requires dedicated facilities for penicillin/cephalosporin but uses different risk framework

rules.requirement: Health-based exposure limits (HBELs) must be used for risk identification in shared facilities per EMA guideline
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA uses 1/1000th therapeutic dose approach

rules.requirement: Separate sampling area required for starting materials
QC laboratories must be separated from production areas; biological, microbiological, and radioisotope labs separated from each other
Water pipes must be sanitised per written procedures with action limits for microbiological contamination
Site Master File required describing GMP-related activities
rules.fda_equivalent: No FDA equivalent document required

rules.requirement: Batch documentation retained for 1 year after batch expiry OR 5 years after QP certification, whichever is longer
rules.fda_equivalent: 21 CFR 211.180 requires 1 year after batch expiry or 1 year after distribution, no 5-year QP rule

rules.requirement: IMP batch documentation retained 5 years after completion/discontinuation of last clinical trial using the batch
Technical Agreements required between contract givers and acceptors
Cross-contamination prevention must use Quality Risk Management approach with health-based exposure limits from toxicological evaluation
Technical and organisational measures for cross-contamination prevention include: dedicated facilities, self-contained areas, campaign manufacturing, validated cleaning with HBEL-based limits
Starting materials must be identity-tested; each container must have identity test unless validated reduced procedure
rules.fda_equivalent: 21 CFR 211.84(d) requires at least one specific identity test per component lot

rules.requirement: Water used in manufacture must comply with European Pharmacopoeia monographs
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA references USP

rules.requirement: Reprocessing permitted only if final product meets all specifications; recovery of materials only if predefined procedure exists
QC department must be independent from Production under person with appropriate qualifications
OOS and OOT investigation procedure required
On-going stability programme required post-marketing: minimum 1 batch per year per strength per primary packaging type
Technical transfer of test methods requires gap analysis vs ICH/VICH, detailed protocol, and documented transfer report
Reference standards must preferably be compendial (European Pharmacopoeia); secondary standards require documented traceability to primary
Written Contract required establishing duties of Contract Giver and Contract Acceptor
QP certification responsibility must be clearly stated in Contract Giver QMS
Contract Acceptor must not subcontract without Contract Giver's prior evaluation and approval
Quality defects must be reported to all concerned Competent Authorities in a timely manner when product recall or abnormal supply restriction may result
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA reporting via MedWatch/Field Alert Reports with different triggers

rules.requirement: Falsification assessment required for all quality defect complaints
rules.fda_equivalent: No specific FDA requirement for falsification screening of complaints

rules.requirement: Mock recall evaluations should be periodically performed, including out-of-office hours scenarios
Self inspections at intervals per pre-arranged programme covering personnel, premises, equipment, documentation, production, QC, distribution, complaints/recalls
Contamination Control Strategy (CCS) required: formal documented strategy covering 16 mandatory elements across entire facility
rules.fda_equivalent: No FDA equivalent documented strategy requirement

rules.requirement: Four-grade cleanroom classification system (A/B/C/D) with mandatory at-rest AND in-operation particle limits
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA uses ISO 14644 classifications without dual at-rest/in-operation states

rules.requirement: Sinks and drains PROHIBITED in Grade A and Grade B areas
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA has no such prohibition

rules.requirement: Minimum 10 Pa pressure differential between adjacent rooms of different grades
Pre-Use Post-Sterilisation Integrity Test (PUPSIT) required for sterilising grade filters
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA does not mandate PUPSIT

rules.requirement: Requalification intervals: Grade A/B every 6 months maximum; Grade C/D every 12 months maximum
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA has no set requalification frequency

rules.requirement: Aseptic Process Simulation (APS/media fill): minimum 3 consecutive initial, then every 6 months per line/shift/process; minimum 5,000-10,000 units; zero growth target
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA guidance less prescriptive on frequency and unit count

rules.requirement: Annual gowning requalification with microbial assessment required
100% container closure integrity testing for fusion-sealed containers <=100mL; visual inspection NOT acceptable
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA does not require 100% CCIT

rules.requirement: Disinfectants and detergents in Grade A and B must be sterile prior to use
Airflow visualization studies required at rest and in operation with video recordings retained
UV irradiation is NOT an acceptable sterilisation method
WFI may be produced by distillation, reverse osmosis, EDI, ultrafiltration, or nanofiltration (aligned with PhEur since 2017)
Daily water sampling from at least one representative point when water used for manufacturing
Personnel disqualification system required based on adverse trends or failed APS; retraining and successful APS participation needed for requalification
Wristwatches, make-up, jewellery, mobile phones prohibited in clean areas
Depyrogenation must achieve minimum 3 log10 endotoxin reduction
Traceability records for human cells/tissues retained for 30 years after product expiry per Directive 2004/23/EC
Dedicated production areas required for live cells capable of persistence and pathogenic organisms
TSE compliance mandatory per EU Note for Guidance
For products with shelf life <=14 days, two-stage QP certification allowed (conditional then final)
Risk assessment required for each computerised system considering patient safety, data integrity, and product quality
rules.fda_equivalent: 21 CFR Part 11 focuses on electronic records/signatures without explicit risk assessment

rules.requirement: Validation documentation and reports must cover entire system lifecycle
Data must be secured by physical or electronic means against damage; stored data checked for accessibility, readability, and accuracy at periodic intervals
Audit trails must be available and convertible to generally intelligible form; entries cannot be altered without recording the previous entry

Qualification stages: User Requirements Specification (URS), Design Qualification (DQ), Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), Installation Qualification (IQ), Operational Qualification (OQ), Performance Qualification (PQ)
Process validation using traditional approach, continuous process verification, or hybrid approach
Cleaning validation must use health-based exposure limits (HBELs) for setting acceptance criteria in shared facilities
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA traditionally uses 1/1000th dose or 10ppm criteria

rules.requirement: Transport verification required for products shipped between manufacturing sites
Each batch must be certified by a QP before release; QP must have university degree in pharmacy, chemistry, biology, or medicine per Article 49 Directive 2001/83/EC
rules.fda_equivalent: No FDA equivalent; QCU head has release authority under 21 CFR 211.22

rules.requirement: QP must personally ensure each batch meets MA requirements and was manufactured/checked in compliance with GMP
For imported products: full qualitative analysis + quantitative analysis of all actives + all tests ensuring quality must be performed in an EU Member State
QP certification allows product to move freely within EU/EEA without re-testing in other Member States
Parametric release only permitted for terminally sterilised products as alternative to sterility testing
Requires specific approval in Marketing Authorisation; competent authority must approve
Reference samples of each batch of finished product retained in final packaging for minimum 1 year after expiry
Starting material reference samples retained for minimum 2 years after release of batch in which used
90% CI for AUC and Cmax ratio must fall within 80.00-125.00% (log-transformed)
Reference product must be authorised in EU/EEA Member State
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA requires US-marketed Reference Listed Drug

rules.requirement: RSABE for highly variable drugs: widening to max 69.84-143.19% permitted for Cmax ONLY, never for AUC
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA allows RSABE for BOTH AUC and Cmax

rules.requirement: Narrow therapeutic index drugs: 90.00-111.11% limits on case-by-case basis
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA uses scaled approach for NTIDs

rules.requirement: BCS biowaiver only for Class I (high solubility, high permeability); NOT for Class III
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA allows BCS Class I AND Class III biowaivers

rules.requirement: Test batch minimum 1/10th production scale or 100,000 units, whichever is greater
Both fasting AND fed BE studies generally required unless waiver justified
Comprehensive physicochemical and biological characterisation using state-of-the-art analytical methods required
Side-by-side quality comparison with reference product at key development milestones using multiple batches
Reference product must be authorised in EU/EEA; non-EEA data supportive only with scientific bridge
HCP must be measured by orthogonal methods (ELISA and mass spec recommended)
Forced degradation comparative studies required: thermal, oxidative, photolytic, pH, mechanical
Three approaches recognised: traditional, continuous process verification, hybrid
Pilot batch minimum 10% of production scale or 100,000 units for solid oral, whichever is greater
Scale-up factor must not exceed 10x from pilot to production
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA does not specify maximum scale-up factor

rules.requirement: Minimum 3 production-scale batches for traditional validation unless otherwise justified
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA moved away from prescriptive batch numbers

rules.requirement: Validation scheme must be submitted in MAA dossier (Module 3); full-scale data may be deferred for standard processes
Non-standard processes (biologics, sterile, MDIs, modified-release, novel tech) require full production-scale validation data at MAA submission
Validation report must be signed by Qualified Person
Novel excipients require full drug-substance-level dossier including manufacturing process, impurity profiling, and toxicology package
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA requires bridge safety assessment, not full NDA-level package

rules.requirement: Excipients must comply with European Pharmacopoeia monographs where they exist
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA references USP-NF

rules.requirement: Certificate of Suitability (CEP) from EDQM accepted as alternative to full description
rules.fda_equivalent: No CEP equivalent; FDA uses DMFs

rules.requirement: TSE/BSE risk assessment mandatory for excipients of animal or human origin

ema_finished_dosage_form.source: EMA Finished Dosage Form Guideline EMA/362427/2017

rules.requirement: Commercial batch for solid oral: minimum 100,000 units unless orphan justification
Process parameters must have target values or ranges, not 'typical' or 'suitable'
Hold times >30 days for solid oral or >24 hours for sterile must be justified with stability data on at least 2 pilot batches
Design Space subject to regulatory approval; movement within is not a change, movement outside requires variation
Multi-tiered ADA assay strategy mandatory: screening (5% false positive rate), confirmatory, neutralizing antibody characterisation
Drug tolerance of ADA assay must exceed therapeutic trough levels
Immunogenicity data for 1 year of treatment normally required pre-authorisation for chronic therapy
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA more flexible on duration

rules.requirement: Brand name AND batch number must be collected for all immunogenicity-related adverse events (traceability)
rules.fda_equivalent: FDA does not mandate batch-level AE traceability

active_substances.source: EU GMP Part II (based on ICH Q7)

rules.requirement: GMP applicable from the defined starting material point through to final API purification and packaging
Written agreement required between API manufacturer and drug product manufacturer defining GMP responsibilities
Reprocessing of API batches failing specifications permitted if predefined procedure; retreatment (different process) requires investigation and regulatory impact assessment
Impurity profiles of API batches compared to reference standard/clinical batches; when patterns change, investigation required
